hawkish_phrases: A majority of participants judged the upside risk to inflation as the greater of these two risks

if this year's higher tariffs were to generate a larger-than-expected or a more-persistent-than-anticipated increase in inflation... it would be appropriate to maintain a more restrictive stance of monetary policy
Several participants emphasized that inflation had exceeded 2 percent for an extended period and that this experience increased the risk of longer-term inflation expectations becoming unanchored
Participants generally expected inflation to increase in the near term
dovish_phrases: A couple of participants preferred to lower the target range for the federal funds rate by 25 basis points at this meeting
excluding tariff effects, inflation was running close to the Committee's 2 percent objective
downside risk to employment had meaningfully increased with the slowing of the growth of economic activity
Several participants noted that their contacts and business survey respondents had reported being reluctant to hire or fire amid elevated uncertainty
if labor market conditions were to weaken materially or if inflation were to come down further... it would be appropriate to establish a less restrictive stance of monetary policy
summary: The Committee voted to hold the federal funds rate at 4-1/4 to 4-1/2 percent with two dissents preferring a 25bp cut (Bowman and Waller), citing elevated but tariff-influenced inflation and balanced risks with majority viewing upside inflation risk as greater concern.
